    • Policy or public engagement activities

This structure allows fellows to strengthen both international academic connections and local impact within their home countries.

Fellowship Benefits

The British Council 90th Anniversary Research Fellowship offers comprehensive academic and financial support.

Benefits include:

Financial Benefits

  1. £2,500 monthly bursary for 12 months
  2. Travel expenses coverage
  3. Support for international research mobility

Academic Benefits

  1. Dedicated office space
  2. University email access
  3. Full library access
  4. Academic mentoring
  5. Participation in seminars and workshops
  6. Opportunities to organize funded academic events

Professional Networking Opportunities

  1. Weekly Fellows’ Lunch community sessions
  2. Work-in-progress seminar series
  3. Interaction with leading scholars
  4. Networking with British Council staff
  5. International academic collaboration opportunities

Research Environment

The Institute for Advanced Studies in the Humanities offers:

  • A historic scholarly environment
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Quiet research spaces
  • A vibrant visiting fellows community
  • Access to world-class research resources

Eligible Research Themes

Research proposals should align with the British Council’s strategic priorities.

Eligible themes may include:

  • Arts and culture
  • Education
  • English language
  • International relations
  • Cultural diplomacy
  • International development
  • Peace building
  • Soft power
  • Humanities research
  • Cross-cultural engagement

Applicants are encouraged to propose innovative and interdisciplinary projects.

Importantly, fellowship proposals should also demonstrate:

  • Relevance to Official Development Assistance (ODA) policy or practice
  • Potential impact in the applicant’s home country or region
  • Plans for public engagement or stakeholder dissemination

Fellowship Objectives

Applicants must explain in their cover letter how their fellowship will contribute to the programme’s objectives, including:

  1. Generating new knowledge that supports British Council strategy
  2. Expanding diverse global research perspectives
  3. Supporting international research capacity building
  4. Strengthening UK-international research partnerships
  5. Promoting knowledge exchange
  6. Increasing visibility and application of research outputs
  7. Supporting the UK research and development agenda

Eligible Countries

Applicants must be based in ODA-recipient countries where the British Council operates.

Eligible countries include:

  • Albania
  • Algeria
  • Argentina
  • Armenia
  • Azerbaijan
  • Bangladesh
  • Bosnia and Herzegovina
  • Botswana
  • Brazil
  • Cambodia
  • Cameroon
  • China
  • Colombia
  • Cuba
  • Egypt
  • Ethiopia
  • Georgia
  • Ghana
  • India
  • Indonesia
  • Iraq
  • Jamaica
  • Jordan
  • Kazakhstan
  • Kenya
  • Kosovo
  • Laos
  • Lebanon
  • Libya
  • Malawi
  • Malaysia
  • Mauritius
  • Mexico
  • Montenegro
  • Morocco
  • Mozambique
  • Myanmar
  • Namibia
  • Nepal
  • Nigeria
  • North Macedonia
  • Pakistan
  • Peru
  • Philippines
  • Rwanda
  • Senegal
  • Serbia
  • Sierra Leone
  • South Africa
  • South Sudan
  • Sri Lanka
  • Sudan
  • Syria
  • Tanzania
  • Thailand
  • Tunisia
  • Türkiye
  • Uganda
  • Ukraine
  • Uzbekistan
  • Venezuela
  • Viet Nam
  • Occupied Palestinian Territories
  • Yemen
  • Zambia
  • Zimbabwe

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following conditions:

Academic Requirements

  • Must hold a PhD
  • Doctorate should normally have been completed within the last seven years
  • Career breaks such as parental leave may be considered

Applicants who have not formally graduated may still apply if:

  • Their viva examination has been successfully completed
  • All thesis corrections have been finalized
  • They can provide proof of completion

Employment Restrictions

Applicants:

  • Must not have held a permanent university position
  • Must not previously have held an IASH fellowship

However:

  • Temporary or short-term academic employment is allowed

Additional Expectations

Applicants should:

  • Demonstrate strong academic achievement
  • Show commitment to knowledge exchange
  • Engage non-academic audiences
  • Connect with University of Edinburgh researchers before applying
  • Demonstrate relevance to the Edinburgh research community

Assessment Criteria

Applications will be reviewed by a panel from the University of Edinburgh and the British Council.

Selection criteria include:

  1. Academic and professional background
  2. Quality of research proposal
  3. Relevance to programme themes
  4. Potential impact and dissemination
  5. Experience engaging public or policy audiences
  6. Commitment to long-term knowledge exchange

Application Timeline

Applicants should carefully note the official timeline:

  • Application Portal Opens: 5 May 2026
  • Applicant Webinar: 26 May 2026
  • Application Deadline: 31 July 2026 at 17:00 BST
  • Selection Results: By end of September 2026
  • Fellowship Start Date: January 2027

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit:

  • Completed application form
  • CV
  • Research proposal
  • Cover letter
  • Two to three confidential references

One referee must confirm successful PhD completion.

All supporting documents should be emailed to:

  • iash@ed.ac.uk

Important Fellowship Conditions

Applicants should understand the following conditions:

  • The fellowship begins only in January 2027
  • No alternative start dates are allowed
  • Fellows must reside at IASH during the 10-month residential phase
  • Fellows must participate actively in institute activities
  • Fellows must deliver at least one seminar presentation
  • Fellows must submit a final research report

No regular teaching duties are required.

Webinar Opportunity for Applicants

Interested applicants can attend an online Q&A webinar organized by the British Council and IASH.

Webinar Date:

  • 26 May 2026

This session will help applicants better understand:

  • Fellowship expectations
  • Eligibility requirements
  • Application preparation
  • Research alignment with programme objectives

 

Final Thoughts

The British Council 90th Anniversary Research Fellowships 2027 provide an outstanding fully funded opportunity for early-career postdoctoral researchers from eligible ODA-recipient countries.

With generous financial support, world-class academic resources, mentorship, and international networking opportunities at the University of Edinburgh, the fellowship offers a powerful platform for advancing impactful global research.

Researchers working in areas related to arts, education, international development, cultural diplomacy, peace building, and humanities should strongly consider applying before the deadline.

How to Apply

Applicants should apply through the official application portal and ensure all documents and references are submitted before the deadline.

 

Apply here

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post British Council 90th Anniversary Research Fellowships 2027 at the University of Edinburgh: Fully Funded 12-Month Fellowship for Early-Career Researchers from ODA Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: LEAP Africa Youth Leadership Development Programme 2026: Apply for Leadership Training and ₦350,000 Community Project Grants in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Young Nigerians passionate about leadership, social impact, advocacy, sustainability, and nation-building now have an exciting opportunity to strengthen their skills and access funding through the LEAP Africa Youth Leadership Development Programme (YLDP) 2026. Designed by LEAP Africa under the Nigeria Youth Futures Fund (NYFF), the Youth Leadership Development Programme is one of Nigeria’s leading youth […]

The post LEAP Africa Youth Leadership Development Programme 2026: Apply for Leadership Training and ₦350,000 Community Project Grants in Nigeria appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young Nigerians passionate about leadership, social impact, advocacy, sustainability, and nation-building now have an exciting opportunity to strengthen their skills and access funding through the LEAP Africa Youth Leadership Development Programme (YLDP) 2026.

Designed by LEAP Africa under the Nigeria Youth Futures Fund (NYFF), the Youth Leadership Development Programme is one of Nigeria’s leading youth empowerment and civic leadership initiatives focused on equipping undergraduates and young changemakers with practical leadership skills, advocacy knowledge, and community development experience.

The programme combines leadership training, mentorship, project development, advocacy engagement, and grant opportunities to support young people who are committed to driving meaningful social change in their communities and across Nigeria.

Participants in the Sustainability Pathway can access grants of up to ₦350,000 to implement community-focused change projects aligned with national development priorities and the thematic focus areas of the Nigeria Youth Futures Fund.

For young Nigerians seeking opportunities to become active citizens, ethical leaders, community builders, and policy advocates, the LEAP Africa Youth Leadership Development Programme offers an impactful platform for personal growth and community transformation.

About LEAP Africa

LEAP Africa (Leadership, Effectiveness, Accountability and Professionalism Africa) is a youth-focused leadership development organization dedicated to raising transformational African leaders.

Over the years, LEAP Africa has become one of the continent’s most recognized organizations promoting:

  • Youth leadership
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Employability
  • Civic engagement
  • Social innovation
  • Community development
  • Active citizenship
  • Health and wellbeing

The organization works to bridge critical gaps in education, leadership, entrepreneurship, and employability by equipping young Africans with practical skills, leadership values, and opportunities for growth.

LEAP Africa’s programmes have impacted thousands of young people across Nigeria and beyond, empowering them to become ethical leaders capable of creating sustainable social change.

What is the Youth Leadership Development Programme (YLDP)?

The Youth Leadership Development Programme is a leadership and civic engagement initiative specifically designed for undergraduates in tertiary institutions across Nigeria.

The programme seeks to equip young people with:

  • Leadership skills
  • Advocacy knowledge
  • Community engagement strategies
  • Social innovation tools
  • Policy awareness
  • Team-building capabilities
  • Sustainable project management experience

YLDP recognizes that Nigerian youth are eager to lead and contribute to national development, but often require the right skills, mentorship, and opportunities to maximize their impact.

The programme therefore serves as a platform to nurture young leaders who are capable of driving change in their communities, institutions, and society.

The Nigeria Youth Futures Fund (NYFF)

The Youth Leadership Development Programme is supported through the Nigeria Youth Futures Fund (NYFF).

The NYFF seeks grant applications from qualified individuals and organizations committed to strengthening Nigeria’s youth ecosystem and advancing youth-driven development.

The fund supports initiatives aligned with key thematic development priorities and focuses on empowering young people as leaders, innovators, and changemakers.

Objectives of the Youth Leadership Development Programme

The programme was created to:

1. Promote Ethical Leadership

Participants are trained to become responsible, accountable, and values-driven leaders capable of influencing positive change.

2. Strengthen Community Development

YLDP equips participants with the tools needed to identify challenges within their communities and implement practical, sustainable solutions.

3. Build Advocacy and Civic Engagement Skills

Participants gain deeper understanding of:

  • Policy advocacy
  • Social change strategies
  • Citizen engagement
  • Community mobilization
  • Public leadership

4. Empower Young People for Nation-Building

The programme encourages youth participation in national development by equipping them with the confidence and competencies needed to contribute meaningfully to society.

Programme Pathways

The Youth Leadership Development Programme features two major pathways for participants.

1. Sustainability Pathway – Hearts and Hands for Change

The Sustainability Pathway focuses on practical community-based projects and sustainable social impact.

How It Works

Participants in this track work in teams of:

  • 5 to 10 people
  • From the same tertiary institution

Each team develops and implements a community change project aligned with the thematic priorities of the Nigeria Youth Futures Fund.

Key Focus Areas of the Sustainability Pathway

Participants receive training and support in:

  • Community engagement
  • Social impact project design
  • Fundraising
  • Project implementation
  • Leadership development
  • Stakeholder collaboration
  • Sustainable development practices

The pathway empowers students to become active changemakers capable of designing solutions that improve their communities.

₦350,000 Grant Opportunity

One of the biggest highlights of the programme is the grant funding available for sustainability teams.

Eligible teams can receive:

Up to ₦350,000

The grant is intended to support the implementation of selected community development projects.

This funding can help participants:

  • Launch community initiatives
  • Organize awareness campaigns
  • Implement sustainability projects
  • Support advocacy activities
  • Execute local development interventions

2. Advocacy Pathway

The Advocacy Pathway focuses on empowering youth voices in policy, governance, and civic participation.

Participants in this pathway develop competencies in:

  • Policy advocacy
  • Leadership communication
  • Public engagement
  • Social mobilization
  • Civic leadership
  • Strategic campaigning

This track is ideal for young people passionate about influencing systems, policies, governance structures, and social transformation.

Why the LEAP Africa YLDP Stands Out

The Youth Leadership Development Programme differs from many youth programmes because it combines:

  • Leadership development
  • Practical implementation
  • Funding opportunities
  • Advocacy training
  • Community transformation
  • Team collaboration
  • Youth empowerment

Rather than focusing only on classroom learning, YLDP emphasizes real-world impact and practical leadership experience.

Benefits of the Programme

Participants in the programme gain access to numerous opportunities and benefits.

1. Leadership Development Training

Participants receive practical leadership training focused on:

  • Ethical leadership
  • Emotional intelligence
  • Communication
  • Teamwork
  • Problem-solving
  • Critical thinking

2. Community Impact Experience

Participants gain hands-on experience implementing projects that address real community challenges.

3. Grant Funding

Eligible sustainability teams can access grants of up to ₦350,000.

4. Networking Opportunities

Participants connect with:

  • Youth leaders
  • Social innovators
  • Community builders
  • Development professionals
  • Change-makers

5. Advocacy Skills

Participants learn how to:

  • Influence policy
  • Mobilize communities
  • Lead campaigns
  • Promote civic participation

6. Capacity Building

The programme equips participants with skills in:

  • Project management
  • Strategic planning
  • Social innovation
  • Community mobilization
  • Stakeholder engagement

Success Stories and Impact

LEAP Africa has consistently impacted thousands of young people through its leadership programmes.

The Youth Leadership Development Programme has already produced remarkable outcomes between 2021 and 2023, supporting young Nigerians in:

  • Community development
  • Youth advocacy
  • Leadership growth
  • Social impact initiatives
  • Civic engagement

The programme continues to empower the next generation of African leaders capable of driving long-term transformation.

Who Should Apply?

The programme is ideal for:

  • Undergraduate students
  • Student leaders
  • Youth advocates
  • Social innovators
  • Young changemakers
  • Community development enthusiasts
  • Civic engagement advocates
  • Sustainability-focused youth teams

It is especially valuable for young Nigerians passionate about leadership and community transformation.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should register through the Official LEAP Africa application platform.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare early and ensure all required information is submitted correctly.

Why Youth Leadership Matters in Nigeria

Nigeria has one of the youngest populations in the world, making youth leadership development critical to national progress.

Programmes like YLDP help equip young Nigerians with:

  • Leadership confidence
  • Civic awareness
  • Development skills
  • Advocacy competencies
  • Community engagement experience

By investing in young people, LEAP Africa contributes to building a stronger and more sustainable future for Nigeria and Africa.

Learn more about the LEAP Africa Youth Leadership Development Programme 2026 HERE.

Conclusion

The LEAP Africa Youth Leadership Development Programme 2026 is more than a training programme. It is a transformational leadership platform designed to empower young Nigerians with the tools, funding, and opportunities needed to become effective leaders and community changemakers.

With practical leadership development, advocacy pathways, sustainability projects, and access to grants of up to ₦350,000, the programme offers exceptional value for young people seeking to create real social impact.

Students and youth teams passionate about sustainability, leadership, civic engagement, and nation-building should strongly consider applying.

Discover more global African, Grant, and Leadership opportunities on OFY.

The post LEAP Africa Youth Leadership Development Programme 2026: Apply for Leadership Training and ₦350,000 Community Project Grants in Nigeria appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
